Top side leakage - The actual specification of the cathode fitting area in the plastic body is 9.5 ± 0.05 mm. However, the dimension in the plastic body is below the specified limit, causing improper cathode fitting. This leads to shrinkage during the sealing process and results in top-side leakage.

bottom side leakage- During the container assembly process, the sealing tool temperature varied by 8°C from the specified bottom sealing temperature of 130+/-5°C. This variation caused shrinkage during sealing, leading to excessive leakage.

Bottom side leakage- The top area of the plastic body has a higher OD, causing tight fitting in the bottom locator. As a result, the plastic body is not fully inserted into the locator, which leads to a sealing height issue and results in bottom-side leakage.
